title: Real-time Patient Dashboard
description: The system shall display each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time on a live dashboard, updating automatically in real time without manual refresh.
rationale: This requirement ensures that authorized hospital staff can monitor the queue status in real time, allowing for efficient management of resources and improved patient care.
business_value: Implementing this requirement will lead to better resource utilization, shorter wait times for patients, and enhanced overall operational efficiency at the hospital.

source.description: As a hospital administrator, I need a live dashboard that displays each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time so that staff can monitor the queue status in real time.

acceptance_criteria: Given the hospital administrator is logged into the system
When the administrator opens the live dashboard
Then the dashboard shows each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time
And the data updates automatically in real time
preconditions: The hospital administrator has a valid login credentials
postconditions: The live dashboard is displayed with up-to-date information on patients' urgency levels and estimated wait times
functional_constraints: The dashboard must update in real time without manual refresh.
Estimated wait times are calculated based on current queue and resource availability.
security_requirements: Access to the live dashboard is restricted to authorized hospital staff only.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system shall provide fast response times for data updates to ensure real-time monitoring.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The live dashboard must be available 24/7 without significant downtime.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to handle an increasing number of patients and staff members without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should be robust, and the interface should be intuitive for authorized users.

edge_cases: What happens if there are no patients in the queue?
How should the system handle sudden spikes in patient arrivals?
What if a patient's urgency level changes during their wait time?
dependencies: The hospital's patient management system to provide real-time data on patient status and resource availability.
A reliable backend service for calculating estimated wait times based on current conditions.

interfaces.name: Live Dashboard Interface
interfaces.description: The user interface for displaying each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time in real-time.

interfaces.name: Patient Management API
interfaces.description: An API to fetch current patient status and resource availability data from the hospital's patient management system.

interfaces.name: Wait Time Estimation Service
interfaces.description: A backend service responsible for calculating estimated wait times based on the current queue and resource availability.

test_cases.description: Verify that the live dashboard displays each patient's urgency level and estimated wait time.
test_cases.expected_result: The dashboard shows each patient's urgency level (critical, high, medium, low) and estimated wait time.

test_cases.description: Confirm that the data on the live dashboard updates automatically in real-time.
test_cases.expected_result: The displayed information on patients' urgency levels and estimated wait times changes as new data becomes available without manual refresh.

test_cases.description: Check that unauthorized users cannot access the live dashboard.
test_cases.expected_result: Access to the live dashboard is denied for unauthorized hospital staff, and an appropriate error message is displayed.

test_cases.description: Validate that the system handles cases where there are no patients in the queue.
test_cases.expected_result: The dashboard displays a message indicating that there are no patients waiting, and all other data fields are blank.

test_cases.description: Test the system's response to sudden spikes in patient arrivals.
test_cases.expected_result: The dashboard updates immediately with the new queue status and recalculated estimated wait times for all patients.
